consumer_boundaries: Consumers read cockpit/ldd/view_model.json as the primary generated artifact.
Mock payloads are examples and must not replace the generated view model.
Raw runtime records are reserved for audit and debug workflows.
Consumers do not infer live prices or execution-valid quotes.
Active rules are read-only risk context, not automated order instructions.
Consumers preserve warnings, data_quality, quote types, source references, and portfolio mode.
Consumers distinguish active positions, closed positions, opportunity cost, rule compliance, risk watch, execution-ready risk rules, and prohibited instruments.
privacy_limitations: The package is internal and not customer-facing.
No role-based permission or field-level authorization system exists.
Exact account identifiers and login details must never be added.
Account values require a future masking and disclosure policy before customer-facing use.
safety_limitations: No live API or market-data connection exists.
User-provided broker and Binance screenshots remain the execution source of truth.
warnings: Future customer-facing consumers require privacy masking and role permissions.
Future API consumers require authentication, authorization, and immutable read-only boundaries.
recommended_next_phase: Vol.5 Phase 5.7 - Consumer Contract Test Matrix and Privacy Boundary
